Back to Projects
Shipped Jul 2026 — Jul 2026

From Messy Data to Database Design — CommBank Data Science Job Simulation

Completed Commonwealth Bank's Forage data science simulation — cleaned and filtered a real transactional dataset, anonymised sensitive customer info, then designed a relational database to track tweets, replies, quote retweets, and mentions.

Challenges

Task 1: Cleaned and filtered a raw, messy dataset before any analysis could begin — closer to real analyst work than a pre-cleaned CSV

Task 2: Masked and anonymised personal/sensitive data, learning practical techniques for protecting customer privacy in a dataset

Task 3/4: Designed a relational database from scratch to track CommBank's tweets, replies, quote retweets, and mentions — using self-referencing keys to avoid redundant tables

Overall, practiced the full pipeline: raw data → clean data → anonymised data → structured schema

What I Learned

-Applied core database design principles (entities, primary keys, relationships) to a real-world social media use case

-Used self-referencing foreign keys to model replies and quote retweets within a single Tweets table, instead of duplicating structure across multiple tables

-Practiced translating an ambiguous business ask ("track everything mentioning us") into a normalized schema that scales

-Reinforced how good schema design up front saves messy joins and data duplication down the line

#Data Analyst #Microsoft Excel#Data Science

A hands-on walkthrough of the data science lifecycle — cleaning, anonymising, and structuring real-world data into a working database

Sarvysh D Hulani
